Pseudocadia anomala is also known to be a part of the larger group of polypore fungi.

intro: Pseudocadia anomala, also known as Pseudocadia anomala, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Rosaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pseudocadia anomala include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pseudocadia anomala include teas, infusions, and decoctions, often used to treat respiratory problems and other conditions associated with inflammation and infection.
